overview: Pamplona, the capital of the Navarre region in northern Spain, is renowned for its vibrant culture and historical significance. Nestled in the picturesque landscape of the Pyrenees foothills, this city is famous for the Running of the Bulls during the San Fermín festival, attracting thousands of visitors each year. With a population of approximately 208,243, Pamplona serves as a cultural and economic hub, blending rich traditions with modern amenities, making it a must-visit destination in Europe.
history: Pamplona was founded in 75 BC by the Roman general Pompey, originally named Pompaelo. It has played a pivotal role throughout history, particularly during the medieval period as a key trading post.
economy: Pamplona's economy is diverse, with significant contributions from sectors such as services, manufacturing, and agriculture. The city is known for its production of quality wines and food products, alongside a growing technology and innovation sector, bolstering its economic landscape.
tourism: Tourism in Pamplona flourishes, especially during the San Fermín festival, where visitors experience the thrill of the Running of the Bulls. Other attractions include the historic old town, the impressive Pamplona Cathedral, and the scenic Citadel, offering a blend of cultural experiences and picturesque views.

best_restaurants.name: Casa Otano
best_restaurants.description: A well-known restaurant serving traditional Navarrese cuisine with a modern twist.

best_restaurants.name: La Cocina Vasca
best_restaurants.description: Famous for its Basque-inspired dishes and fresh local ingredients.

best_restaurants.name: Restaurante Europa
best_restaurants.description: Offers a fine dining experience with a focus on seasonal and regional specialties.

best_restaurants.name: Bodega de la Catedral
best_restaurants.description: A charming spot near the cathedral, known for its tapas and local wines.

best_restaurants.name: Bar Gaucho
best_restaurants.description: A popular bar and grill offering a lively atmosphere and delicious grilled meats.

weather_summary: Pamplona experiences mild, rainy winters and warm summers, with occasional heat waves during peak summer months.

faqs.question: What time zone is Pamplona in?
faqs.answer: Pamplona is in the Europe/Madrid time zone.

faqs.question: Does Pamplona observe daylight saving time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pamplona observes daylight saving time, typically shifting forward one hour in late March.

faqs.question: What is the population of Pamplona?
faqs.answer: The population of Pamplona is approximately 208,243.

faqs.question: When does sunrise typically occur in Pamplona?
faqs.answer: Sunrise in Pamplona typically occurs around 7:30 AM in the summer and around 8:30 AM in the winter.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Pamplona?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit Pamplona is during the San Fermín festival in July or in the spring and fall for milder weather.
